title: Backup ISP Link Saturated After Prefix-List Update Leaks Outbound Traffic

ticket.summary: Since approximately 02:30 last night, Meridian Retail's enterprise network has experienced widespread slowness for outbound internet traffic (cloud SaaS apps, card-processing API calls, general browsing) enterprise-wide, with users reporting timeouts and high latency. The primary 1 Gbps ISP-Alpha transit link looks fine and lightly loaded, but NOC noticed the 100 Mbps backup ISP-Beta link, normally reserved for a partner data center route, is now running close to 100% utilization. A prefix-list change was deployed on edge-rtr-beta at 02:15 as part of onboarding a new partner subnet.

topology.description: Meridian Retail (AS 65001) is multi-homed with two transit providers: ISP-Alpha (AS 65100), a 1 Gbps primary link terminated on edge-rtr-alpha, and ISP-Beta (AS 65200), a 100 Mbps secondary link terminated on edge-rtr-beta that is intended only to carry a small set of routes to the partner data center (198.51.100.0/24) plus act as a low-preference backup default path. edge-rtr-alpha and edge-rtr-beta both run iBGP to core-rtr01, which makes the final best-path selection for outbound traffic based on local-preference learned from each edge router. An inbound route-map on edge-rtr-beta (RM-BETA-IN) is supposed to set local-preference 200 (higher than Alpha's default 150) only for the specific partner prefix-list PL-BETA-PARTNER, and local-preference 50 (lower than Alpha) for everything else, so ISP-Beta is only preferred for the partner block. At 02:15 an engineer updated PL-BETA-PARTNER to add the newly onboarded partner subnet 198.51.101.0/24.

links.label: iBGP, learns routes with local-pref 150 default
iBGP, learns routes via RM-BETA-IN local-pref policy
unintended: now carrying general outbound traffic

logs.content:
Jul 14 02:15:41 edge-rtr-beta %SYS-5-CONFIG_I: Configured from console by jchen (CHG0042205 - add partner subnet 198.51.101.0/24 to PL-BETA-PARTNER)
Jul 14 02:15:41 edge-rtr-beta CHANGE: ip prefix-list PL-BETA-PARTNER seq 5 permit 198.51.100.0/24
Jul 14 02:15:41 edge-rtr-beta CHANGE: ip prefix-list PL-BETA-PARTNER seq 10 permit 198.51.101.0/24
Jul 14 02:15:41 edge-rtr-beta CHANGE: ip prefix-list PL-BETA-PARTNER seq 15 permit 0.0.0.0/0 le 32
Jul 14 02:15:42 edge-rtr-beta %ROUTE-MAP-6-REEVAL: route-map RM-BETA-IN re-evaluated, 184213 prefixes now matched by clause 10 (was 2)

02:10 jchen: pushing prefix-list update for partner onboarding, adding 198.51.101.0/24, should be low risk
02:16 jchen: change applied, partner subnet reachable, closing change window
08:41 noc-shift2: multiple 'slow internet' tickets coming in from HQ and both DCs, checking WAN links now

Jul 14 03:02:18 edge-rtr-beta %LINK-3-UTIL_HIGH: GigabitEthernet0/1 (ISP-Beta uplink) utilization 92% sustained over 5 min (link is provisioned at 100 Mbps)
Jul 14 03:02:18 edge-rtr-beta %QUEUE-4-DROP: output queue drops on GigabitEthernet0/1, 14203 packets dropped in last 5 min interval

cli_outputs.output:
ip prefix-list PL-BETA-PARTNER: 3 entries
   seq 5 permit 198.51.100.0/24
   seq 10 permit 198.51.101.0/24
   seq 15 permit 0.0.0.0/0 le 32

Note from ticket history: seq 15 was meant to read 'deny 0.0.0.0/0 le 32' as an explicit catch-all deny (prefix-lists deny by default when no match occurs, but a prior audit added an explicit permit-any as a placeholder that was never removed). The new seq 10 entry was inserted correctly, but the pre-existing placeholder line at seq 15 was not caught during review.

route-map RM-BETA-IN, permit, sequence 10
  Match clauses:
    ip address prefix-list PL-BETA-PARTNER
  Set clauses:
    local-preference 200
route-map RM-BETA-IN, permit, sequence 20
  Match clauses:
  Set clauses:
    local-preference 50

cli_outputs.command: show ip bgp neighbor 203.0.113.254 received-routes | count
cli_outputs.output:
Total prefixes received from ISP-Beta (AS 65200): 184,215
Prefixes matching route-map clause 10 (local-pref 200): 184,213
Prefixes matching route-map clause 20 (local-pref 50): 2
(Expected: ~2 prefixes at local-pref 200 for the partner block, remainder at local-pref 50)

BGP routing table entry for 8.8.8.0/24
Paths: (2 available, best #2)
  65100 (via edge-rtr-alpha, iBGP)
    Local-pref 150, valid, internal
  65200 (via edge-rtr-beta, iBGP)
    Local-pref 200, valid, internal, best

Note: destination 8.8.8.0/24 has no relation to the partner data center, yet is now being preferred out ISP-Beta due to higher local-preference.

5 minute input rate 210000000 bits/sec, 5 minute output rate 95000000 bits/sec
(Link capacity: 1,000,000,000 bits/sec — running at under 10% of primary link's capacity while backup link is saturated)

monitoring.notes: Grafana shows ISP-Beta uplink utilization jump from a steady ~8% baseline (only partner traffic) to 92-98% starting 2026-07-14 02:16, one minute after the prefix-list change (CHG0042205) was applied. In the same window, ISP-Alpha's utilization dropped from its normal ~35% baseline to under 10%. Application latency dashboards show p95 latency for outbound SaaS calls increasing from ~40ms to 800ms+ starting at the same timestamp, with packet loss alerts beginning at 03:02 once the Beta link's queue started dropping packets — all three signals (Beta utilization, Alpha utilization, latency/loss) pivot at the same moment, pointing at a routing preference change rather than a capacity or hardware issue.

hints: The primary 1 Gbps ISP-Alpha link is nearly idle while the 100 Mbps ISP-Beta backup link — which should only carry partner data center traffic — is saturated. Compare local-preference values for a random, non-partner destination on both edge routers.
On edge-rtr-beta, check how many prefixes the inbound route-map RM-BETA-IN's high-local-preference clause is matching, versus how many it should be matching for just the partner subnets.

The prefix-list PL-BETA-PARTNER has a leftover placeholder entry, 'seq 15 permit 0.0.0.0/0 le 32', that matches every prefix and was never removed — combined with the new seq 10 entry, it causes RM-BETA-IN to set local-preference 200 on essentially all 184,000+ routes learned from ISP-Beta instead of just the ~2 partner routes, so core-rtr01 now prefers ISP-Beta for almost all outbound traffic.

solution.root_cause: The inbound prefix-list PL-BETA-PARTNER on edge-rtr-beta contained a leftover placeholder entry ('seq 15 permit 0.0.0.0/0 le 32') that matches every possible prefix. When the 02:15 change added the new partner subnet 198.51.101.0/24, this pre-existing catch-all entry was not caught in review, so route-map RM-BETA-IN's clause 10 (set local-preference 200) ends up matching essentially all ~184,000 prefixes received from ISP-Beta instead of only the two partner routes. Since local-preference 200 beats ISP-Alpha's default of 150, core-rtr01 now prefers the 100 Mbps ISP-Beta backup link for nearly all outbound traffic, saturating it and causing widespread latency and packet loss.
solution.fix: Remove the catch-all 'permit 0.0.0.0/0 le 32' placeholder entry from PL-BETA-PARTNER (or replace it with an explicit deny) so route-map RM-BETA-IN clause 10 only matches the intended partner subnets (198.51.100.0/24 and 198.51.101.0/24), restoring local-preference 50 for all other routes learned from ISP-Beta.
